summary: Linked Marth/Roy neutral-B implementation file `ftMs_SpecialN.c` at 3.29 KB with a 100% report across all listed functions, deleting the old 1014-line assembly file and moving the object from asm/nonmatching to matching C. The PR also cleaned up typed headers and field names used by this file, added Doxygen prototype stubs for library helpers it calls, and converted many raw motion-state IDs/flags and magic constants to project enums/macros where possible.
key_files: asm/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/ftMs_SpecialN.s - deleted assembly source for the linked file
src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/ftMs_SpecialN.c - matched C implementation for Marth/Roy neutral-B start/loop/end ground and air states
src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/ftMs_SpecialN.h - prototypes updated to use `Fighter_GObj*` and `ft/forward.h`
configure.py - object switched to matching C
obj_files.mk - object switched to C object path
config/GALE01/scratches.txt - scratch/decomp.me status entries updated for the linked functions
src/melee/lb/lb_00B0.dox - added prototypes including `lb_8000B1CC` world-position helper
src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.dox - added prototypes including `lb_800119DC` aesthetic wind effect helper
what_changed: Deleted `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/ftMs_SpecialN.s` and enabled `src/melee/ft/chara/ftMars/ftMs_SpecialN.c` as a matching object in `configure.py` and `obj_files.mk`.
Matched the neutral-B entry, start, loop, end, IASA, physics, and collision callbacks for ground and air variants; the PR report shows every listed function at 100.00% with score 0.
Replaced raw user-data access patterns with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` in the C implementation.
Replaced raw motion-state numbers such as `0x155`, `0x156`, `0x157`, `0x158`, `0x159`, `0x15A`, `0x15B`, and `0x15C` with `ftMs_MS_*` motion-state enum names where shown in the diff.

Replaced some raw motion flags with named flags (`Ft_MF_None`, `Ft_MF_KeepSfx`, `Ft_MF_SkipColAnim`) and factored other large bitmasks into local `static u32 const mf` values with `@todo Named flags` notes.
Renamed Marth/Roy special-N motion variable `x0` to `cur_frame`, including the reset in `ftMs_SpecialN_80136730` and loop/end damage calculations.
Renamed `MarsAttributes.xC` to `specialn_friction`, clarifying the friction divisor used by ground and air neutral-B enter functions.
Factored duplicated ground/air logic into static inline helpers: `doStartAnim`, `doLoopAnim`, `doLoopIasa`, and `inlineA0`.
Kept matching-sensitive padding and stack-shape workarounds visible with `PAD_STACK(4 * 2)` and dummy `u64` block variables in functions where the diff shows them.
Added `.dox` declarations for library functions used by this file, notably `lb_8000B1CC` and `lb_800119DC`, and added short comments for known helper behavior.
Updated `ftMs_SpecialN.h` prototypes from generic `HSD_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*`, while retaining address comments.
smart_moves: Used small static inline helpers to collapse nearly identical ground/air variants while preserving matched output, especially for start animation transition, charge loop handling, IASA B-release behavior, and end animation behavior.
Introduced semantic field names (`specialn_friction`, `cur_frame`) exactly where the linked file made their roles clear, instead of leaving all accesses as anonymous `xC`/`x0`.
Converted motion state calls from hardcoded IDs to `ftMs_MS_*` enum names, making future fighter-state work easier to audit without changing behavior.

For large unnamed motion flag masks, avoided pretending to know every bit; the code uses local `mf` constants and explicit `@todo Named flags`, preserving matching while marking a future cleanup point.
Replaced the old pointer arithmetic over hit capsules with typed `fp->x914[i]` iteration and `ARRAY_SIZE(fp->x914)`, improving struct knowledge while still matching according to the report.
Used `HSD_PAD_B`, `GA_Ground`, `FtPart_HipN`, `HitCapsule_Enabled`, `true`/`false`, and `NULL` in places where the asm constants mapped cleanly to existing project names.
Added library `.dox` prototypes for external helpers instead of relying on undeclared calls or placeholders in this now-linked file.
decomp_lessons: When both ground and air variants are structurally identical, a `static inline` helper with a callback parameter can reduce duplication and still match if scoped carefully.
A file-link PR can be a good moment to rename fields whose meaning is demonstrated by repeated use, but renames should be narrow and backed by clear usage evidence.
Keep named enums/macros for motion states and simple flags, but leave complex bitmasks as explicit bit expressions with a `@todo` rather than guessing names.
Dummy scoped variables (`u64 _`, `_0`, `_1`) and `PAD_STACK` may be necessary to preserve stack layout in matched fighter callbacks; keep them isolated so future cleanup can identify them.
Typed GObj aliases (`Fighter_GObj*`) in headers help communicate expected object kind and reduce generic `HSD_GObj*` ambiguity for fighter callbacks.
For hitbox loops, prefer typed array iteration (`fp->x914`, `ARRAY_SIZE`) over raw address stepping once the struct layout supports it.
When linking a file that calls underdocumented library helpers, add `.dox` prototypes and comments near the library module so future files can reuse them.
naming_conventions: Marth/Roy file prefix remains `ftMs_`, with ground/air callback names such as `ftMs_SpecialNStart_Anim` and `ftMs_SpecialAirNStart_Anim`.
Unknown helper functions still retain address-style names like `ftMs_SpecialN_80136A1C`, `ftMs_SpecialN_801371FC`, and `ftMs_SpecialN_801373B8` when no descriptive name is evidenced.

Motion states use `ftMs_MS_*` enum names, for example `ftMs_MS_SpecialNStart`, `ftMs_MS_SpecialAirNStart`, `ftMs_MS_SpecialNLoop`, `ftMs_MS_SpecialAirNLoop`, `ftMs_MS_SpecialNEnd0/1`, and `ftMs_MS_SpecialAirNEnd0/1`.
The special-N per-move frame counter is named `cur_frame`.
The attribute at old offset/member `xC` is named `specialn_friction`.
Callbacks in the header use `Fighter_GObj* gobj` rather than generic `HSD_GObj* gobj`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed the asm file only after the report showed `ftMs_SpecialN.c` at 100.00% with score 0 across all functions.
Used `PAD_STACK(4 * 2)` in enter/physics functions to match stack layout that previously appeared as unused byte arrays.
Used scoped dummy `u64` locals inside `doLoopAnim` and `inlineA0` to influence stack/register layout without changing semantics.
Maintained exact float behavior where needed, including `60 * deg_to_rad` in loop wind effects and an explicit `1.0471975803375244140625f` constant in end animation wind effect.
Kept callback assignment forms matching-sensitive: `fp->x21EC = ftMs_SpecialN_80136730`, `fp->accessory4_cb = ftMs_SpecialN_801365A8`, and `fp->accessory4_cb = ftMs_SpecialN_8013666C`.
Used callback-parameterized inlines (`HSD_GObjEvent cb`) so ground/air variants share C source while producing the expected specialized code.
Factored large motion flags into `static u32 const mf` local constants; this can help control immediate materialization while avoiding opaque hex literals.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that name the large neutral-B motion flag masks marked `@todo Named flags`.
Search for uses of `specialn_friction` to confirm whether the field applies only to Marth/Roy neutral-B entry speed scaling.
Search for other files still using `mv.ms.specialn.x0` or equivalent anonymous counters that could be renamed to `cur_frame`.
Look for later PRs that replace address-style helpers `ftMs_SpecialN_80136A1C`/`80136A7C`/`801371FC`/`801372A8`/`80137354`/`801373B8` with descriptive names.
Compare other fighter charge-move implementations for reuse of the `doLoopAnim`/`doLoopIasa` callback-inline pattern.
Search for additional `.dox` consumers of `lb_8000B1CC` and `lb_800119DC` to refine their signatures and names.
